biomes.description:
Gandalf is the grey pilgrim, a wanderer of hidden roads and quiet counsel. He walks the edge between shadow and hope, guiding the small and the humble because he knows their courage can outlast any sword. He trusts in patience, in wisdom earned by travel, and in the spark that lights when others have given in to fear.

His road stretches beyond the Shire into the wider lanes of Eriador and the scattered strongholds of the Free Peoples. He gathers trusted companions, listens for uneasy rumors, and searches old ground for relics and allies that still matter long after the first road is taken.
biomes.deckIdentity: Deck identity: Grey and uncommitted — spread thin, armies light. No road yet chosen.

variants.description:
Mithrandir, pilgrim of the West, passes like an old song through Rivendell, Lothlorien, and the hidden paths where the Wise still keep watch against the ancient Shadow. In him abide memory, mercy, and the secret fire set against a darkness older than the kingdoms of Men, and where he treads, veils are lifted and the designs of the Enemy are read before they ripen to ruin.

Under Mithrandir, the game turns toward revelation, warding, foresight, and spellcraft wielded with patient precision rather than open might.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: The grey wanderer walks every road — all grown deep, and his host many-armed: Elves, scouts, and fleets. runs swift; the price of every open road is steep.

variants.description:
Stormcrow they name him in the troubled halls of kings, for his coming is the whisper before the war-horn, the black wing against a paling sky. He is seen where Rohan wavers, where Gondor keeps weary watch, and where the old roads of the North begin once more to feel the breath of Angmar, Isengard, and Mordor upon their stones.

This variant carries the weight of vigilance, strained loyalties, bitter choices, and endurance beneath a darkening sky.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: The Stormcrow's web of lands and roads produces in abundance — needing no supply line. His armies are thin and sorcery slight; he wins by presence, not force.

variants.description:
At Bag End and along the Bree-road, in fireworks, whispers, and the sudden knock at dusk, the Disturber of the Peace unsettles the comfortable world. From those small provocations spring journeys, riddles, unlikely fellowships, and the turning of humble lives toward deeds that will outlast towers and crowns.

This variant leans into the Shire, Bree-land, and the long road through Eriador: hobbit-scale mischief, roadside wonders, strange meetings, and the little interruptions that reroute an entire tale.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: A mischief deck: saturates every shadow, archers ready where blades would be heard. Hobbits and wanderers turn Bree-land into disruption. No sorcery , no heavy arms — only the deed done before it is suspected.

variants.description:
Tharkun is the name the Dwarves remember: friend of the House of Durin, walker in the deeps, and counselor in halls where stone keeps faith longer than flesh. He knows the grief buried in Khazad-dum, the splendor of Erebor restored, and the peril that lies ever beside gold, mithril, and the pride of mountain kings.

This variant turns toward hidden wealth, mountain fortitude, and alliances tempered in the dark under stone.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: Gandalf as dwarf-friend: towers high, flows self-sustaining from deep veins, Dwarven axemen grinding hard endurance under stone. No sorcery — what he builds, he builds with toil.

variants.description:
Clothed in white and returned from death, the White Rider comes from Fangorn like a judgment upon despair. He breaks the pride of Saruman, scatters the poison of Wormtongue, and stands in the last defence of the West as hidden wisdom is transfigured into open command beneath the sunlit banners of hope.

This variant is one of cleansing, renewal, fearless command, and the sudden turning of darkness beneath a greater light.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: Returned white authority: dominant, heavy horse and fleets of Gondor and Rohan at his back, lords rallied wide . The mage's art is spent — he leads now by command, not craft.

biomes.description:
Saruman is the master of Orthanc, once the chief of the wise, now the architect of his own dominion.His voice bends courts, his mind measures every weakness, and his ambition refuses to wait for permission. He builds in silence, gathering strength behind fair words, and he intends to remake the world in the image of his will.

His design begins with a careful reach into the courts of Rohan, the securing of a pliant voice, and the quiet unearthing of power long buried. From Orthanc he turns influence into force and raises the first ranks of his war.
biomes.deckIdentity: Deck identity: The white before the breaking — each open, already turning. No road yet chosen.

variants.description:
Saruman the White stands in the majesty of Orthanc, chief of the Istari, master of lore, ring-craft, and the long devices of the Wise. His voice is a weapon finer than steel, entering the counsels of kings and bending proud hearts with honeyed reason until obedience seems wisdom and surrender seems peace.

This variant favors influence, manipulation, and command exercised with polished restraint.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: Voice, art, and argument — all keen, with events reshaping the board. His host is numerous but common-born , and his territories spread thin.

variants.description:
Saruman the Many-Coloured shatters the white that once signified order and reveals the glittering fracture of pride. In that figure are gathered ring-lore, engines, subtle arts, and the cold brilliance of a mind that would rival the makers of old, yet all are bent now toward dominion, display, and the blasphemous imitation of a power once sent to oppose the Shadow.

This variant leans into grand sorcery, layered effects, and the dangerous splendor of power unbound by humility.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: The widest host and richest harvest — cavalry no other master fields so deep, in flood, grand sorcery and many colours. is scarce; the forge runs cold.

variants.description:
The White Hand rises over Isengard like a blasphemous banner, and beneath it the world is remade in smoke, iron, and ruin. Fangorn falls to the axe, the furnaces thunder without ceasing, and from the black pits march Uruk-hai and wolf-riders, forged for the breaking of Helm's Deep and the humiliation of the Mark.

This variant drives forward with industry, relentless pressure, and the hard discipline of Isengard at war.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: Isengard's forged war machine: drive iron-will infantry, furnaces thunder, Uruk discipline makes aggression systematic. No sorcery ; what he lacks in art, he pays in .

variants.description:
Sharkey is the name they give him in the Shire, and he wears it like a snarl. Stripped of tower and art and title, what remains is spite: a bitter will that turns the Shire's fields into quarries, pens the hobbits in lockholes, and burns what it cannot own. He does not conquer — he poisons. He does not command armies — he directs ruffians, extortion, and the slow erasure of everything pleasant about a people who never harmed him.

This variant operates without sorcery and without great armies. It wins through intimidation, pipe-weed monopoly, occupation of settlements, and the grinding cruelty of petty authority over a land that has no walls.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: No towers, no spells , no cavalry — Sharkey rules through and the fear they carry. flow from an occupied Shire; his ruffians are cheap and mean.

biomes.description:
Sauron is the Dark Lord returned, a will of iron that has never forgotten its claim. From Barad-dur he bends the lands through fear and patience, drawing all things toward a single purpose. He does not need love or loyalty, only obedience, and he will take it by force or by dread.

The hunt begins in the shadow of Minas Morgul, where the trail is secured and a relic of power is drawn from cursed ground. From there the march moves toward the Black Gate, where the first disciplined hosts take shape under a hard command.
biomes.deckIdentity: Deck identity: Every shadow he has ever worn still breathes — waking, cavalry restless. No shape yet fixed.

variants.description:
The Dark Eye burns above Barad-dur like a star of malice that never sets, sleepless over Gorgoroth, the Black Gate, and the dead roads beneath Ephel Duath. It is dread made watchful, a will that peers through palantiri, dreams, and trembling rumor until no secrecy feels safe and every hidden movement seems already betrayed.

This variant is built on exposure, fear, and a suffocating dominion that narrows the enemy's every choice.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: Nothing hidden survives — towers above all peers, captains answer as one across twenty domains . Heavy infantry holds every field; he is slow to mount .

variants.description:
The Deceiver walks in the memory of the Second Age: Annatar, Lord of Gifts, whispering to the smiths of Eregion; corrupter of Celebrimbor's great labor; and dark counselor to the pride of Numenor in its final blasphemy. Fair-seeming, patient, and terrible in wisdom misused, he raises no banner at first, but teaches the mighty to forge their own chains until kingdoms drown, temples burn, and the bent world itself bears witness to his fraud.

This variant leans into deceit, seduction, false gifts, and the hidden mastery won long before the sword is drawn.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: The Deceiver's tongue outpaces every sword; and armies flood from his dominion, PCs spanning wide. Little sorcery and few great events — his power is bought, not conjured.

variants.description:
The Necromancer broods in Dol Guldur, and Greenwood the Great withers into Mirkwood beneath the shadow of his hidden throne. From that hill of sorcery crawl spiders, wraiths, and corrupted things, while fear seeps through root and branch until the very forest seems to remember some older terror returning from forgotten ages.

This variant favors haunted pressure, monstrous servants, dark sorcery, and corruption that lingers long after the first blow.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: From Dol Guldur, seeps endlessly; dark art and heavy infantry grind the forest to shadow. No agents and no emissaries — he does not parley; he waits.

variants.description:
The Shadow of the East stirs where the sun rises over the Sea of Rhûn and the red war-spears of the Easterlings gather beneath whispers older than their tribal kings. Sauron has walked there in secret, leaving gifts of iron and fear among the horse-lords and wagon-folk, and now the eastern steppes breed armies that look westward with hungry patience. From the lands beyond the maps of the Wise, a tide of bondage and blade gathers, and the Free Peoples feel the first cold wind of a storm born far beyond their walls.

This variant is built on eastern hordes, dark diplomacy, and the patient raising of vast armies through emissary craft and whispered threats.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: The shadow rides east on tireless horse — cavalry and agents spreading darkness before armies know it has arrived. No spells and no envoys ; he moves too fast for councils.

variants.description:
The Iron Crown rises in the Witch-realm of Angmar, where the Witch-king wears cold dominion over the ruined hills of Eriador. He does not come with open war at first, but with deception, plague, and the slow breaking of hope among the Men of Arnor and Cardolan. From the mountain holds of the North, Orcs and Trolls are bred like a pestilence, while slavers and false counsel gnaw at the edges of a kingdom that no longer trusts its own shadow. Dark sorcery flows from Carn Dûm like a black river, and the crown upon the Witch-king's brow is the seal of a winter that will not end until the last hearth in the West is cold.

This variant favors deceit, recruitment through terror, slaving, monstrous hordes from the mountains, and sorcery that bends the will before the blade is drawn.
variants.deckIdentity: Deck identity: The Iron Crown forges strength in iron and commands columns that do not break . No spy and few diplomats ; it does not negotiate — it occupies.
